sentences.en: River deltas are difficult places for map makers.
sentences.zh: 对制图者来说,河流三角洲是很难处理的地方。

grammar.note: 主语 River deltas,系动词 are,表语 difficult places;for map makers 介词短语作状语,表示"对……而言"。

sentences.en: The river builds them up, the sea wears them down; their outlines are always changing.
sentences.zh: 河流使三角洲不断淤积扩大,海水又将其侵蚀削减;它们的轮廓总在变化。

grammar.note: builds them up 与 wears them down 结构对仗,构成一对反义动词短语;分号后第三个分句用现在进行时 are always changing,与 always 连用强调反复不断的变化。

sentences.en: The changes in China's Pearl River delta, however, are more dramatic than these natural fluctuations.
sentences.zh: 然而,中国珠江三角洲的变化比这些自然波动更为剧烈。

grammar.note: 主干 The changes are more dramatic than...;however 为插入语;in China's Pearl River delta 介词短语作后置定语修饰 changes。

sentences.en: An island six kilometres long and with a total area of 1248 hectares is being created there.
sentences.zh: 一座长六公里、总面积1248公顷的岛屿正在那里被建造出来。

grammar.note: 主语 An island 带两个后置定语:形容词短语 six kilometres long 和介词短语 with a total area of...;谓语 is being created 为现在进行时的被动语态,表"正在被建造"。

sentences.en: And the civil engineers are as interested in performance as in speed and size.
sentences.zh: 而土木工程师们对这座岛的性能与对建造速度和规模同样在意。

grammar.note: as interested in performance as (interested) in speed and size,第二个 as 之后承前省略了 interested;be interested in 为固定搭配。

sentences.en: This is a bit of the delta that they want to endure.
sentences.zh: 这是三角洲中他们希望能长久留存的一部分。

grammar.note: that they want to endure 为定语从句;endure 此处作不及物动词"持久、留存"解——工程师希望这片人造陆地不像三角洲其他部分那样变动消失。

sentences.en: The new island of Chek Lap Kok, the site of Hong Kong's new airport, is 83% complete.
sentences.zh: 赤鱲角新岛——香港新机场的所在地——已完成了83%。

grammar.note: the site of Hong Kong's new airport 是主语 The new island of Chek Lap Kok 的同位语,补充说明其身份;主干为 The island is 83% complete。

sentences.en: The giant dumper trucks rumbling across it will have finished their job by the middle of this year and the airport itself will be built at a similarly breakneck pace.
sentences.zh: 在岛上隆隆驶过的巨型自卸卡车将在今年年中之前完成任务,机场本身也将以同样飞快的速度建成。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 将来完成时
grammar.note: rumbling across it 现在分词短语作后置定语修饰 trucks;will have finished 将来完成时,与 by the middle of this year 搭配表"到某时间点前完成";and 连接第二分句,用被动 will be built。

sentences.en: As Chek Lap Kok rises, however, another new Asian island is sinking back into the sea.
sentences.zh: 然而,就在赤鱲角不断升起的同时,另一座亚洲新岛却正沉回海里。

grammar.note: As 引导时间状语从句,表"随着/当……的同时";主句现在进行时 is sinking 与从句 rises 形成"一升一沉"的对照;however 为插入语。

sentences.en: This is a 520-hectare island built in Osaka Bay, Japan, that serves as the platform for the new Kansai airport.
sentences.zh: 这是一座建在日本大阪湾、面积520公顷的岛屿,充当新关西机场的平台。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: built in Osaka Bay 过去分词短语作后置定语(被动含义"被建在");that serves as... 定语从句进一步修饰 island;serve as 意为"充当,用作"。

sentences.en: Chek Lap Kok was built in a different way, and thus hopes to avoid the same sinking fate.
sentences.zh: 赤鱲角以不同的方式建造,因此有望避免同样下沉的命运。

grammar.note: and thus 连接两个谓语:was built(被动)与 hopes(主动);thus 为副词"因此";sinking 现在分词作定语修饰 fate。

sentences.en: The usual way to reclaim land is to pile sand and rock on to the seabed.
sentences.zh: 填海造地的常用方法是把沙和石堆到海床上。

grammar.note: to reclaim land 不定式作后置定语修饰 way;表语 to pile... 同为不定式短语,构成 "The way to do A is to do B" 句型。

sentences.en: When the seabed oozes with mud, this is rather like placing a textbook on a wet sponge: the weight squeezes the water out, causing both water and sponge to settle lower.
sentences.zh: 当海床渗满淤泥时,这就像把一本教科书放在湿海绵上:重量把水挤出来,使水和海绵都往下沉。

grammar.type: 时间状语从句 + 分词结果状语
grammar.note: When 引导时间状语从句;冒号后的分句解释这一比喻;causing... 现在分词短语作结果状语,内含 cause sth to do 结构。

sentences.en: The settlement is rarely even: different parts sink at different rates.
sentences.zh: 沉降很少是均匀的:不同部位以不同的速度下沉。

grammar.note: settlement 此处是工程术语"沉降",不是"定居点";rarely 为半否定副词"很少";冒号后的分句具体解释前句。

sentences.en: So buildings, pipes, roads and so on tend to buckle and crack.
sentences.zh: 因此建筑物、管道、道路等往往会弯折开裂。

grammar.note: So 表结果;and so on 意为"等等";tend to do "往往会";buckle 与 crack 两个动词并列。

sentences.en: You can engineer around these problems, or you can engineer them out.
sentences.zh: 你可以用工程手段绕开这些问题,也可以从设计上把它们彻底消除。

grammar.note: engineer 此处作动词"用工程方法处理";engineer around"绕开(带着问题设计)"与 engineer out"(通过设计)消除"形成对照。

sentences.en: Kansai took the first approach; Chek Lap Kok is taking the second.
sentences.zh: 关西机场采用了第一种方法;赤鱲角正在采用第二种。

grammar.note: 分号连接两个对照分句;took 一般过去时(已完成的选择)与 is taking 现在进行时(正在进行)形成时态对照;the second 后省略了 approach。

sentences.en: The differences are both political and geological.
sentences.zh: 两者的差异既有政治层面的,也有地质层面的。

grammar.note: both A and B 连接两个并列的表语形容词 political 与 geological。

sentences.en: Kansai was supposed to be built just one kilometre offshore, where the seabed is quite solid.
sentences.zh: 关西机场原本打算建在离岸仅一公里处,那里的海床相当坚实。

grammar.note: was supposed to "本应/原计划",暗示后来发生了改变;where 引导非限制性定语从句,修饰地点 one kilometre offshore。

sentences.en: Fishermen protested, and the site was shifted a further five kilometres.
sentences.zh: 渔民们提出抗议,于是选址又向外挪了五公里。

grammar.note: 第二分句用被动语态 was shifted;a further five kilometres "又五公里",further 修饰数量,整体看作一个单位故用不定冠词 a。

sentences.en: That put it in deeper water (around 20 metres) and above a seabed that consisted of 20 metres of soft alluvial silt and mud deposits.
sentences.zh: 这一挪使它处在更深的水域(约20米),而且下方的海床由20米厚松软的冲积淤泥沉积构成。

grammar.type: 并列地点状语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: put it in... and above... 两个介词短语并列,说明新位置;that consisted of... 定语从句修饰 seabed;consist of "由……构成"。

sentences.en: Worse, below it was a not-very-firm glacial deposit hundreds of metres thick.
sentences.zh: 更糟的是,它下面还有一层数百米厚、不太坚实的冰川沉积物。

grammar.note: 地点状语 below it 提前,主语 a glacial deposit 后置,构成完全倒装;hundreds of metres thick 形容词短语后置作定语;Worse 为句首评注性状语。

sentences.en: The Kansai builders recognised that settlement was inevitable.
sentences.zh: 关西机场的建造者认识到,沉降是不可避免的。

grammar.note: that 引导宾语从句作 recognised 的宾语;主句为过去时,从句相应用过去时 was。

sentences.en: Sand was driven into the seabed to strengthen it before the landfill was piled on top, in an attempt to slow the process; but this has not been as effective as had been hoped.
sentences.zh: 在填埋料堆上去之前,人们先把沙桩打入海床来加固它,试图减缓沉降过程;但效果不如预期。

grammar.type: 复合句(被动 + 时间从句 + 同级比较)
grammar.note: 主句被动 was driven;to strengthen it 目的状语;before 引导时间状语从句;in an attempt to do "试图";分号后 not as effective as had been hoped 是同级比较的否定式。

sentences.en: To cope with settlement, Kansai's giant terminal is supported on 900 pillars.
sentences.zh: 为了应对沉降,关西机场巨大的航站楼由900根柱子支撑。

grammar.type: 不定式目的状语 + 被动
grammar.note: To cope with... 句首不定式短语作目的状语;主句为被动语态 is supported on。

sentences.en: Each of them can be individually jacked up, allowing wedges to be added underneath.
sentences.zh: 每根柱子都可以单独用千斤顶顶起,以便在下面加入楔块。

grammar.type: 情态动词被动式 + 分词结果状语
grammar.note: can be jacked up 情态动词的被动式;allowing... 现在分词短语作结果状语,内含 allow sth to be done 结构。

sentences.en: That is meant to keep the building level.
sentences.zh: 这样做是为了让建筑保持水平。

grammar.note: be meant to do "旨在,目的是";keep + 宾语 + 形容词(level)构成宾语补足语,level 此处是形容词"水平的"。

sentences.en: But it could be a tricky task.
sentences.zh: 但这可能是件棘手的活儿。

grammar.note: could 表可能性推测"可能";主系表结构。

sentences.en: Conditions are different at Chek Lap Kok.
sentences.zh: 赤鱲角的情况则不同。

grammar.note: 简单的主系表结构;at Chek Lap Kok 为地点状语。承上启下,由关西转入赤鱲角。

sentences.en: There was some land there to begin with, the original little island of Chek Lap Kok and a smaller outcrop called Lam Chau.
sentences.zh: 那里一开始就有一些陆地——原有的赤鱲角小岛,和一块名叫榄洲的更小的露岩。

grammar.type: there be 句型 + 同位语
grammar.note: to begin with "起初,本来";逗号后的名词短语是 some land 的同位语,具体说明是哪两块地;called Lam Chau 过去分词短语修饰 outcrop。

sentences.en: Between them, these two outcrops of hard, weathered granite make up a quarter of the new island's surface area.
sentences.zh: 这两块由坚硬风化花岗岩构成的露岩,加起来占了新岛表面积的四分之一。

grammar.note: Between them 此处意为"两者合计";make up "构成,占";weathered 过去分词作定语"风化的"。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, between the islands there was a layer of soft mud, 27 metres thick in places.
sentences.zh: 遗憾的是,两岛之间有一层软泥,有些地方厚达27米。

grammar.type: there be 句型 + 后置形容词短语
grammar.note: 地点状语 between the islands 前置;27 metres thick in places 形容词短语后置,补充说明 mud;in places "在某些地方"。

sentences.en: According to Frans Uiterwijk, a Dutchman who is the project's reclamation director, it would have been possible to leave this mud below the reclaimed land, and to deal with the resulting settlement by the Kansai method.
sentences.zh: 据该项目的填海总监、荷兰人弗兰斯·尤特维克说,本来可以把这些软泥留在填海土地下面,再用关西的办法处理由此产生的沉降。

grammar.type: 虚拟语气 + 同位语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: a Dutchman... 是 Frans Uiterwijk 的同位语,内含 who 引导的定语从句;it 是形式主语,真正主语是 to leave... and to deal with... 两个并列不定式;would have been possible 对过去的虚拟——事实上没有这么做。

sentences.en: But the consortium that won the contract for the island opted for a more aggressive approach.
sentences.zh: 但赢得这座岛建造合同的财团选择了更大刀阔斧的方案。

grammar.note: that won the contract for the island 定语从句修饰主语 consortium,主干为 the consortium opted for...;opt for "选择"。

sentences.en: It assembled the world's largest fleet of dredgers, which sucked up 150m cubic metres of clay and mud and dumped it in deeper waters.
sentences.zh: 它集结了世界上最大的挖泥船队,吸走1.5亿立方米的黏土和淤泥,倾倒到更深的海域。

grammar.note: which 引导非限制性定语从句,修饰 fleet of dredgers;从句内 sucked up 与 dumped 两个谓语并列;150m 即 150 million(1.5亿)。

sentences.en: At the same time, sand was dredged from the waters and piled on top of the layer of stiff clay that the massive dredging had laid bare.
sentences.zh: 与此同时,又从海里挖出沙子,堆在被大规模疏浚裸露出来的硬黏土层上。

grammar.type: 被动并列 + 定语从句(过去完成时)
grammar.note: was dredged and (was) piled 两个被动谓语并列;that... had laid bare 定语从句修饰 clay,先行词作 lay bare 的宾语;had laid 过去完成时,表"在堆沙之前已被裸露"。

sentences.en: Nor was the sand the only thing used.
sentences.zh: 而且用到的还不只是沙子。

grammar.note: 否定词 Nor 置于句首引起部分倒装(was the sand...);used 过去分词作后置定语修饰 thing。

sentences.en: The original granite island which had hills up to 120 metres high was drilled and blasted into boulders no bigger than two metres in diameter.
sentences.zh: 原有的花岗岩岛上有高达120米的山丘,被钻孔爆破成直径不超过两米的巨石块。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 被动并列谓语
grammar.note: which had hills... 定语从句修饰主语 island;was drilled and blasted 两个被动谓语并列;no bigger than... 形容词短语后置修饰 boulders;in diameter "直径"。

sentences.en: This provided 70m cubic metres of granite to add to the island's foundations.
sentences.zh: 这提供了7000万立方米的花岗岩,用来充实岛的地基。

grammar.type: 简单句 + 不定式定语
grammar.note: to add to... 不定式短语作后置定语修饰 granite,表用途;70m 即 70 million(7000万)。

sentences.en: Because the heap of boulders does not fill the space perfectly, this represents the equivalent of 105m cubic metres of landfill.
sentences.zh: 由于石块堆不能把空间填得严丝合缝,这相当于1.05亿立方米的填埋料。

grammar.note: Because 引导原因状语从句;the equivalent of "相当于……的量"。

sentences.en: Most of the rock will become the foundations for the airport's runways and its taxiways.
sentences.zh: 大部分石料将成为机场跑道和滑行道的地基。

grammar.note: 主谓宾结构;runways 与 taxiways 并列作介词 for 的宾语。

sentences.en: The sand dredged from the waters will also be used to provide a two-metre capping layer over the granite platform.
sentences.zh: 从海里挖出的沙子还将用来在花岗岩平台上铺一层两米厚的覆盖层。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 将来时被动
grammar.note: dredged from the waters 过去分词短语作后置定语修饰 sand;will be used to do "将被用来做";two-metre 复合形容词中 metre 用单数。

sentences.en: This makes it easier for utilities to dig trenches - granite is unyielding stuff.
sentences.zh: 这使公用设施单位更容易开挖沟槽——花岗岩可是坚硬难凿的东西。

grammar.note: make it easier for sb to do:it 是形式宾语,真正宾语是不定式 to dig trenches;破折号后补充解释原因。

words.def: 公用事业(水电煤等设施)

sentences.en: Most of the terminal buildings will be placed above the site of the existing island.
sentences.zh: 大多数航站楼建筑将建在现有岛屿的原址上方。

grammar.note: will be placed 一般将来时的被动语态;existing 现在分词作定语"现有的"。

sentences.en: Only a limited amount of pile-driving is needed to support building foundations above softer areas.
sentences.zh: 只需要少量打桩,来支撑较软区域上方的建筑地基。

grammar.type: 被动 + 不定式目的状语
grammar.note: Only 修饰主语 a limited amount of pile-driving;is needed to do 被动语态接目的;pile-driving 为动名词复合词"打桩"。

sentences.en: The completed island will be six to seven metres above sea level.
sentences.zh: 建成后的岛将高出海平面六到七米。

grammar.note: completed 过去分词作定语"建成的";six to seven metres above sea level 作表语,表高度。

sentences.en: In all, 350m cubic metres of material will have been moved.
sentences.zh: 总共将有3.5亿立方米的材料被搬运。

grammar.note: will have been moved 将来完成时的被动语态,表"到完工时将已搬运完";In all "总计"。

sentences.en: And much of it, like the overloads, has to be moved several times before reaching its final resting place.
sentences.zh: 而且其中很多材料像多余的堆料一样,要搬好几次才能到达最终的安放位置。

grammar.note: like the overloads 介词短语作插入性状语;has to be moved 被动;before reaching = before it reaches,介词后接动名词。

sentences.en: For example, there has to be a motorway capable of carrying 150-tonne dump-trucks; and there has to be a raised area for the 15,000 construction workers.
sentences.zh: 例如,得有一条能承载150吨自卸车的公路;还得有一处供15000名建筑工人使用的垫高区域。

grammar.type: there be 句型(has to be)
grammar.note: there has to be "必须有";capable of doing 形容词短语后置修饰 motorway;raised 过去分词作定语"垫高的"。

sentences.en: These are temporary; they will be removed when the airport is finished.
sentences.zh: 这些都是临时设施;机场建成后就会被拆除。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 时间状语从句
grammar.note: 分号连接两个分句;when 引导时间状语从句,遵循"主将从现":主句 will be removed,从句用一般现在时 is finished。

sentences.en: The airport, though, is here to stay.
sentences.zh: 不过,机场本身是要长久留下的。

grammar.note: though 作插入副词"不过";be here to stay 为习语,意为"将长期存在下去"。

sentences.en: To protect it, the new coastline is being bolstered with a formidable twelve kilometres of sea defences.
sentences.zh: 为了保护机场,新海岸线正在用长达12公里、颇具规模的海防工事加固。

grammar.type: 目的状语 + 现在进行时被动
grammar.note: To protect it 句首不定式作目的状语;is being bolstered 现在进行时被动;a formidable twelve kilometres 把"12公里"看作一个整体,故可用不定冠词 a。

sentences.en: The brunt of a typhoon will be deflected by the neighbouring island of Lantau; the sea walls should guard against the rest.
sentences.zh: 台风的主要冲击将由邻近的大屿山岛挡开;其余的则由海堤来防御。

grammar.type: 并列句(被动 + 情态动词)
grammar.note: the brunt of "……的主要冲击力";will be deflected 被动语态;should 表预期"应当能";guard against "防御,提防"。

sentences.en: Gentler but more persistent bad weather - the downpours of the summer monsoon - is also being taken into account.
sentences.zh: 较温和但更持久的坏天气——夏季季风带来的暴雨——也被考虑在内。

grammar.type: 破折号同位语 + 进行时被动
grammar.note: 两个破折号之间是 bad weather 的同位语;主语中心词 weather 为单数,故谓语用 is;take into account "考虑到"的被动进行式。

sentences.en: A mat-like material called geotextile is being laid across the island to separate the rock and sand layers.
sentences.zh: 一种叫土工布的席垫状材料正被铺满全岛,用来隔开岩石层和沙层。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 进行时被动
grammar.note: called geotextile 过去分词短语作后置定语修饰 material;is being laid 现在进行时被动;to separate... 目的状语。

sentences.en: That will stop sand particles from being washed into the rock voids, and so causing further settlement.
sentences.zh: 这将阻止沙粒被冲进岩石空隙、进而造成进一步沉降。

grammar.note: stop sth from being done 中的动名词为被动式;and so causing... 与 being washed 并列,同属 from 之后——即阻止"被冲入空隙并因此造成沉降"这一连串结果。

sentences.en: This island is being built never to be sunk.
sentences.zh: 这座岛的建造目标,就是永不沉没。

grammar.type: 否定不定式作目的状语
grammar.note: never to be sunk 为否定的被动不定式作目的状语;sunk 是 sink 的过去分词。全文以此句点题收束。
